2606.11596.title: Model-Based and Data-Driven Hierarchical Control and Topology Co-Design for Robust Networked Systems
2606.11596.paper_summary: In this paper, we consider a class of networked systems comprising an interconnected set of linear subsystems, disturbance inputs, and performance outputs. Using dissipativity theory, we first propose a model-based hierarchical control design strategy to ensure the closed-loop networked system is dissipative from its disturbance inputs to performance outputs. This involves designing local controllers for each subsystem to enforce local dissipativity guarantees, which are then exploited to co-design distributed global controllers and the interconnection topology to enforce global dissipativity guarantees while optimizing interconnection topology costs. The overall design process requires only solving a sequence of linear matrix inequality (LMI) problems, thereby retaining compositionality and decentralizability while avoiding non-convex, iterative design processes that are inefficient and centralized. This model-based hierarchical control design strategy assumes the knowledge of the subsystem dynamics, which may not hold in many real-world networked systems. Motivated by this, we also propose a data-driven hierarchical control design strategy that assumes only the availability of rich input-state-output trajectory data from the subsystems. The proposed data-driven design process assumes that the unknown disturbances affecting the subsystem dynamics are bounded by a quadratic matrix inequality (relaxing conventional bounds) and accounts for this by using the matrix S-lemma. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed model-based and data-driven hierarchical control designs is illustrated for a networked system representing a DC microgrid, with the aim of enforcing robust (dissipative) voltage regulation and current sharing.
2606.11596.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們考慮一類由互聯的線性子系統、擾動輸入和性能輸出組成的網絡系統。<lb/>利用耗散理論，我們首先提出一種基於模型的分層控制設計策略，以確保閉環網絡系統從其擾動輸入到性能輸出是耗散的。<lb/>這涉及為每個子系統設計局部控制器，以強制執行局部耗散性保證，然後利用這些保證共同設計分佈式全局控制器和互聯拓撲，以強制執行全局耗散性保證，同時優化互聯拓撲成本。<lb/>整體設計過程僅需解決一系列線性矩陣不等式（LMI）問題，從而保持組合性和去中心化，同時避免效率低下且集中化的非凸迭代設計過程。<lb/>這種基於模型的分層控制設計策略假設已知子系統動態，但在許多現實世界的網絡系統中可能並不成立。<lb/>因此，我們還提出了一種數據驅動的分層控制設計策略，僅假設可獲得來自子系統的豐富輸入-狀態-輸出軌跡數據。<lb/>所提出的數據驅動設計過程假設影響子系統動態的未知擾動受到二次矩陣不等式的界限（放寬傳統界限），並通過使用矩陣S-引理來考慮這一點。<lb/>最後，所提出的基於模型和數據驅動的分層控制設計在代表直流微電網的網絡系統中得到了有效性展示，旨在強制執行穩健（耗散）電壓調節和電流共享。
2606.11596.author: Shirantha Welikala et.al.
2606.11596.authors: Shirantha Welikala, Zihao Song, Hai Lin, Panos J. Antsaklis
